channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Among Us Story: On Guard Is A Closer Look At The Lives Of The Crewmates - Interview
item.content:encoded:
After the surprise drop of the Among Us TV Show last week, the Among Us announcements just kept going at the Summer Game Fest Showcase. Among Us Story: On Guard is a new title taking place in the Among Us universe, where, in order to train to better spot impostors, you play as a crewmate who undergoes virtual reality training in a strange single-player mystery title.

The Demo began from the game's opening, where Management announces that the Green Crewmate will be taking part in simulation training to be able to better detect Imposters. Once inside, you take on the role of Guard and very quickly discover that Cook has been murdered.

Immediately getting walked in on by two others, Guard suddenly has the whole ship against him , believing that he is the imposter.

The game becomes equal parts murder mystery and minigames, where the player might have to move around the room to dodge the attention of any other crewmates, use the vents to navigate to a behind a locked door, or use classic Among Us minigames like a well-timed card swipe to lock or unlock a door.

Progression through the story felt like a classic mystery, with Guard talking to himself out loud (sometimes in a noir-esc writing style) about how he needs to search for clues, and how he might be able to get them.

We didn't get to see much of the story in our brief time playing the game, but what was there was lighthearted fun, and just enough of a puzzle to make you question what kind of conundrum would be around the next corner. It was also impressive to see just how quickly a Crewmade with an appropriate name and outfit could so quickly seem fully realized.

Speaking with Forest Willard, CEO and Co-founder of Innersloth, I was interested to know where the idea for this kind of narrative mystery game came from. "We grew from three to thirty people, and we were all working on Among Us in a live-service cycle. You know, going from release to release. I wanted to break the teams apart so that we could have a pit stop game."

The aim for this 'pit stop' game was to be a small title that could be made and released in a shorter frame of time. "It allows for a cooling-off period for employees."

At a company meeting we had the idea of 'what if you were just a crewmate doing crewmate stuff?'

"Then we started going down the rabbit hole. You would still be doing tasks, right? The tasks might be a little bit more interesting or something, and then maybe you don't have to worry about being the imposter; you can just have an imposter present and experience that. It just made sense from there to make a detective mystery out of it."

I joked about the other ways we might see Crewmates going about their daily lives like a Trauma Center-style game for the Doctor with an Among Us approach to surgery. "It's not something I can confirm or deny, but it is something we want to expand upon and see what genre fits for the different roles that we've created."

With the release of Among Us on Paramount+ and now Among Us Story: On Guard, giving us more personality to the different crewmates seen in Among Us, I was interested to know if there were any worries about crewmate colors starting to be typecast or whether their 'personalities' would be pigeonhole?

"We've been really good about it. In general, we treat canon as 'everything in the world is canon,' including each game you play of Among Us. That's your canon session, and those are different crewmates from every other session. So this is just an exploration of 'this is one more thing that can happen."

Willard expanded upon the idea of 'everything is canon' to say that more content, like the TV show or future spin-off games, is "one more tool in your belt. If you want to write a fanfic or whatever, then go for it."

As to the expected length of Among Us Story: On Goard Willard told me that there was obviously going to be some fluctuation in time depending on how quickly you are to solve puzzles, but that Innersloth is expecting about two to five hours of playtime.

Among Us Story: On Guard doesn't have an announced release date, but there will be a demo available to try out on June 15th. It will be available on Steam and Nintendo Switch, and is currently already available to wishlist on Steam.

Among Us Story: On Guard was previewed at Summer Game Fest 2026

Andrew has written Video Game and Entertainment news, reviews, and guides for 10+ years. As Senior Content Manager, he assists in creating and editing… More about Andrew



https://techraptor.net/gaming/features/among-us-story-on-guard-is-closer-look-at-lives-of-crewmates-interview
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
